title: KryptoniteUserStory
description: Schema for a fully specified user story or spike in the kryptonite workflow. Every item must conform to this schema before the spec can be generated.

type.description: feature = standard implementation story. spike = research task that produces knowledge/decisions to inform other stories. Spikes must run before the stories that depend on them.

party.description: The actor/role this story belongs to (must match a defined party from Phase 4). For spikes, use 'system' or the party most relevant to the research.

repo.description: Name of the repository this story belongs to (must match a `name` in the project's repos.json). Cross-repo stories are split into per-repo sub-stories during Phase 8 (see Cross-Repo Story Auto-Split).

as_a.description: The actor role, e.g. 'admin'. For spikes: 'developer' or 'team'.

i_want.description: The action/capability desired. For spikes: the research question.

so_that.description: The motivation/business value. For spikes: what decision this unblocks.

acceptance_criteria.description: Testable conditions. For features: verifiable behaviors. For spikes: questions that must be answered.

description.description: What must be true for this item to pass.

method.description: How to verify this DOD item. curl = HTTP request check. chrome_mcp = browser-based verification. test_suite = run test command. file_exists = check a file was produced (for spikes).

oneOf.description: The exact shell command or URL string. Used when method is 'curl' (full curl invocation, ${APP_URL} as base allowed), 'test_suite' (test runner invocation, e.g. 'npm test -- --grep US-001'), or 'file_exists' (path relative to the repo). NOT used for chrome_mcp.
Structured steps for chrome_mcp validation. Each step is an action object. See the action enum and per-action keys below.

expect.description: Expected result. e.g. '201', 'PASS', 'file exists', or a pattern to match.

definition_of_done.description: Concrete, verifiable items that define completion. Each item includes HOW to validate it automatically. If an item cannot be validated via curl, chrome_mcp, test_suite, or file_exists, it must be rewritten until it can.

priority.description: Business priority. Critical = blocks other work or is a launch requirement. High = important for MVP. Medium = desirable. Low = nice to have.

dependencies.description: List of story IDs that must be completed before this story can be implemented. Spikes that inform a story must be listed as dependencies. Empty array if no dependencies.

estimated_complexity.description: Implementation complexity estimate. Trivial = config change or one-liner. Simple = single file, clear path. Moderate = multiple files, some decisions. Complex = cross-cutting, multiple components. Very complex = architectural changes or novel problems.

has_mock.description: Whether this story has a UI component that needs a visual mockup. Set explicitly during Phase 8 (true for visual stories, false otherwise) — required across all phase gates so Phase 8 and Phase 10 agree. When true, mock_phase and mock_approved must also be set.

mock_phase.description: Two-phase mock system (added in kryptonite 0.2.0). Foundational mocks define the visual DNA and run sequentially with 3 options each; detail mocks inherit and run in parallel. Required when has_mock === true.

mock_approved.description: Whether the mock for this story has been approved by the user.

mock_path.description: Repo-relative path to the approved mock HTML for this story (e.g. 'mocks/US-058.html'). Written by the Phase 8 mock workflow when a mock is approved.

mock_options.description: Identifiers of the option variants generated for this mock (e.g. ['a','b','c']) shown in the /compare view. Recorded by the Phase 8 mock workflow.

mock_choice.description: Which mock option the user selected (e.g. 'a'). Recorded by the Phase 8 mock workflow.

design_notes.description: Free-form notes captured during mock approval describing the chosen layout/treatment for this story's content area. Inherited by Designer agents and the spec generator.

touches_registry_files.description: Files this story must modify that are also modified by other stories in the same wave (registries, aggregator manifests, locale catalogs, GraphQL schema entry points). Listed here — not in the task's regular file_paths — so the parallel-group file-conflict check excludes them; the wave's shared_registry_files entry handles reconciliation at end-of-wave. See references/plan-assembly.md.

amended.description: Whether this story was modified after plan approval. Set to true when acceptance_criteria or definition_of_done are changed during execution.

changed_at.description: ISO timestamp of when the amendment was made

amendment_history.description: History of amendments made to this story after plan approval.

wave.description: Wave index this story is assigned to during Phase 11 plan generation. Stories at wave N may only depend on stories at waves < N.

parallel_group.description: Identifier for the parallel group this story belongs to within its wave. Stories sharing a parallel_group are dispatched concurrently; stories in different groups within the same wave may also run in parallel but are tracked separately for file-conflict reasoning.

status.description: Execution-time state machine. See execution-protocol.md for transition rules.

commit_sha.description: SHA of the commit that implemented this story (recorded by the Coder agent).

implemented_by.description: Identifier of the agent / model that implemented this story.

started_at.description: ISO timestamp of when execution began.

merged_at.description: ISO timestamp of when the story's patch was applied onto the apply_target (git am --3way).

completed_at.description: ISO timestamp of when the story reached `done`.

attempts.description: Number of QA/Review cycles for this story. Escalate to user after 3.

chromeMcpStep.description: One step in a chrome_mcp DOD or user_journey walk. Same shape used by plan-schema.json user_journeys.steps[]. Keep these in sync.

action.description: navigate: load url. click: click selector. fill: type value into selector. wait: pause for timeout_ms. wait_for: wait until selector is visible (timeout_ms optional). assert_text: selector contains/equals expected. assert_count: selector matches min/max/exact. assert_visible / assert_not_visible: presence check on selector. assert_url: current URL contains/equals expected. assert_attribute: selector's `attribute` contains/equals expected. screenshot: capture (name optional). resize_page: set viewport via width/height. evaluate_script: run `script` in page context, optionally assert via expect.

url.description: Used by navigate; absolute or relative.

selector.description: CSS selector. Required by click, fill, wait_for, assert_text, assert_count, assert_visible, assert_not_visible, assert_attribute.

value.description: Used by fill; the text to type.

expect.description: Generic expected-value placeholder (used by evaluate_script and as a fallback for asserts).

attribute.description: Used by assert_attribute; the DOM attribute name (e.g. 'aria-disabled').

script.description: Used by evaluate_script; JavaScript executed in the page context.

name.description: Used by screenshot; output file name suffix.

description.description: Optional human-readable note for this step. Ignored by the runner; useful for review.

note.description: Alias for description. Either may be used.
